Fix NIfTI orientation: respect Fortran memory order on read/write

The nifti crate returns Fortran-order ndarray, but we were flattening
and reshaping as C-order, scrambling voxel positions. Fix read_nifti
to convert to standard layout via into_dimensionality + as_standard_layout,
and fix write_nifti to iterate in Fortran order (first dim varies fastest)
as required by the NIfTI spec.

commit.message:
fix: serialize datetime to ISO strings in all model to_dict() methods (#201)

## Summary

Fixes #196 — datetime serialization error in `KlineModel.to_dict()`

## Problem

Binance klines extraction jobs were failing with:
```
Object of type datetime is not JSON serializable
```
Because `model_dump()` was returning `datetime` objects instead of ISO
strings.

## Solution

Added `mode='json'` to `model_dump()` in `KlineModel.to_dict()`:
```python
# Before
return self.model_dump(exclude={"id"})
# After
return self.model_dump(exclude={"id"}, mode="json")
```

## Changes

- `models/kline.py`: Added `mode='json'` to `to_dict()`
- `tests/test_models.py`: Added
`test_kline_to_dict_datetime_serialization` test

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `KlineModel.to_dict()` uses `model_dump` with `mode='json'` to
serialize datetime to ISO strings
- [x] Unit tests verify datetime serialization works correctly
- [ ] All klines extraction jobs complete successfully with records
written to Data Manager (verified via deployment)

---------

commit.message:
Add configurable fallback image display modes for terminals without bitmap support (#117)

* Initial plan

* Add UnicodeGlyphImage class and DisplayMode support to TImage for pseudo image fallback

- Add UnicodeGlyphImage class in casciian.bits that converts bitmap images to
  Unicode character representations (half-blocks, quadrants, sextants, braille)
  using median cut dithering, adapted to work with ImageRGB instead of BufferedImage
- Add DisplayMode enum to TImage with BITMAP, BLOCKS, UNICODE_HALVES,
  UNICODE_SEXTANTS, UNICODE_QUADRANTS, UNICODE_SIXDOT, UNICODE_SIXDOTSOLID
- Update TImage.sizeToImage() to apply display mode conversion
- Add getter/setter for DisplayMode in TImage
- Add rgbAverage utility method to ImageUtils for computing average color over
  a rectangular region of an ImageRGB

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix quadrant glyph mapping, improve variable naming, add unit tests

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Palette alpha handling: force pixels opaque since ImageRGB has no alpha channel

The Palette constructor inherited from the BufferedImage-based code checked
for alpha == 0xFF and turned non-opaque pixels black. Since ImageRGB stores
plain RGB values without the alpha byte (e.g. SixelDecoder uses 0xFF0000
not 0xFFFF0000), all pixels were being treated as transparent and turned
black. This broke UNICODE_QUADRANTS, UNICODE_SEXTANTS, UNICODE_SIXDOT,
and UNICODE_SIXDOTSOLID display modes.

Fix: OR in 0xFF000000 to force all pixels opaque instead of checking alpha.

Added regression tests using RGB values without alpha byte.

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add automatic fallback to UNICODE_HALVES when backend has no image protocol

When TImage displayMode is BITMAP but the backend does not support any
image protocol (no sixel, no Casciian/Jexer image protocol), cells were
being stored as bitmap images that the terminal could not render, resulting
in a black image.

Added Backend.isImageProtocolSupported() to detect whether the backend
can render bitmap image cells natively. Implemented in ECMA48Terminal
(checks sixel || jexer), ECMA48Backend, HeadlessBackend, TWindowBackend,
and MultiBackend.

TImage.sizeToImage() now auto-falls back to UNICODE_HALVES when BITMAP
mode is requested but the backend lacks image protocol support.

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove Palette and Glyph Mapping functionality from `UnicodeGlyphImage` to streamline the codebase. Clean up related classes, methods, and enums across the project.

* Add casciian.ECMA48.imageFallbackDisplayMode system property

Add a configurable system property to control the fallback display mode
when the terminal doesn't support image protocols. Values: "halves"
(default, UNICODE_HALVES) and "solid" (BLOCKS).

Also fix broken test file that referenced removed methods.

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix fallback image growing/shrinking on terminal resize

When the terminal doesn't report cell size directly (no CSI 16t),
cell size is estimated from window pixels / window chars. On resize,
the estimate can change, triggering sizeToImage to re-break the image
into a different cell grid — making the fallback image appear to grow
or shrink.

For non-BITMAP modes (BLOCKS, UNICODE_HALVES), cells contain text
characters (colored block glyphs) that display correctly regardless
of cell size. Skip re-rendering on cell size changes during draw();
only re-render when explicitly requested (initial load, setImage(),
setDisplayMode()).

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cell size estimation stability on terminal resize

When the terminal doesn't report cell size via CSI 16t, the cell
size is estimated from widthPixels/windowWidth. On resize, CSI 14t
responses can overwrite widthPixels with new window pixel dimensions,
changing the estimated cell size and causing fallback images (and
newly opened ones) to grow/shrink.

Fix: in the resize handler, latch the current estimated cell size
into textWidthPixels/textHeightPixels on first resize when CSI 16t
hasn't been received. This makes getTextWidth()/getTextHeight()
return a stable value regardless of future widthPixels changes.

Also removes the TImage sizeToImage() workaround that skipped
re-rendering for non-BITMAP modes during draw(), since the root
cause is now fixed at the terminal level.

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cell size stability: don't recalculate when widthPixels unchanged

When the terminal doesn't respond to CSI 14t, widthPixels stays at
the default 640.  Previously getTextWidth() computed
widthPixels/windowWidth dynamically, so terminal resizes changed
the estimated cell size (e.g. 640/80=8 to 640/120=5), causing
fallback images to grow/shrink.

Fix: track whether the terminal has actually reported pixel
dimensions via CSI 14t (widthPixelsReportedByTerminal flag).
When it hasn't, cache the initial cell size estimate and return
it on all subsequent calls, keeping it stable across resizes.
When it has, continue computing dynamically.

Also conditionally apply the synthetic widthPixels update in the
resize handler only when the terminal has reported pixel dimensions.

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cell size stability on resize for all terminal types

Always cache the cell size estimate in getTextWidth()/getTextHeight()
instead of computing widthPixels/windowWidth on every call. The cache
is computed once at startup and only updated when a fresh CSI 14t
response arrives (in the CSI 4t handler). This eliminates the race
between widthPixels (updated asynchronously by CSI responses) and
windowWidth (updated by SIGWINCH/stty) that caused the cell size to
fluctuate on terminal resize.

Remove the synthetic widthPixels update from the resize handler
and the widthPixelsReportedByTerminal flags, which are no longer
needed.

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments: null-guard setDisplayMode, clamp negative coords in rgbAverage

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Carlos Ramirez <crramirez@gmail.com>

Fix SP setup screen: is_me was always 0 for player 0

In fillPlayers(), 'is_me = 0' at line 1276 was outside the if/else
chain, unconditionally overwriting 'is_me = 1' that was just set for
slot 0 (the human player). This caused the civ dropdown, team button,
and color button to never activate for the first player row in single
player mode.

The decomp (scr_mps.cpp.decomp @ 0x004A37D0) has bVar15=false only
inside the else branch (for non-zero slots), not after the if/else.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Detect circular dependencies in factory-based service registrations at runtime (#124331)

## Summary

Fixes issue #88390 - Deadlock when configuring
ApplicationInsightsServiceOptions due to circular dependencies through
factory functions.

## Problem

When services are registered using factory functions that call
`IServiceProvider.GetService()`, circular dependencies can cause
deadlocks at runtime when the same lock is acquired recursively in
`CallSiteRuntimeResolver.VisitRootCache()`.

## Solution

Implemented re-entrance detection in `VisitRootCache` using a
`[ThreadStatic]` HashSet to track call sites currently being resolved on
each thread. When a circular dependency is detected (attempting to
resolve a call site already in the resolution chain), an
`InvalidOperationException` is thrown with a clear error message instead
of deadlocking.

### Changes Made

1. **CallSiteRuntimeResolver.cs**: Added re-entrance detection
- Added `[ThreadStatic] HashSet<ServiceCallSite>` to track call sites
being resolved on current thread
- Uses shared `ReferenceEqualityComparer.Instance` from CoreLib
(leveraging IEqualityComparer contravariance)
   - Modified `VisitRootCache()` to:
     - Add call site to tracking set before resolution
- Check if call site is already being resolved (circular dependency)
     - Remove call site from tracking set in finally block
     - Throw clear exception if circular dependency detected

2. **Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.csproj**: Reference shared
ReferenceEqualityComparer
- Added compile reference to
`$(CoreLibSharedDir)System\Collections\Generic\ReferenceEqualityComparer.cs`
   - Uses well-tested shared implementation instead of custom code
- Available for non-.NETCoreApp target frameworks via conditional
compile

3. **Test Coverage**: Improved tests for factory-based circular
dependencies
- `FactoryCircularDependency_DetectedAtResolutionTime` - verifies
detection at service resolution
- `FactoryCircularDependency_NotDetectedByValidateOnBuild` - documents
that ValidateOnBuild cannot detect factory cycles
   - Tests verify proper error messages with service type names
   - Fixed unused variable warnings by using discard operator

## Design Decisions

- **Runtime detection**: Detects circular dependencies at resolution
time, not during ValidateOnBuild
- **Thread-local tracking**: Uses `[ThreadStatic]` to avoid cross-thread
interference
- **Reference equality**: Uses shared `ReferenceEqualityComparer` from
CoreLib via contravariance for type-safe tracking
- **No behavioral changes**: Doesn't change singleton lifetime semantics
or cause side effects
- **Clear error messages**: Uses existing
`SR.CircularDependencyException` resource string
- **Code reuse**: Leverages shared CoreLib implementation

## Benefits

- **Prevents Deadlocks**: Circular dependencies throw exceptions instead
of causing deadlocks
- **No Side Effects**: Doesn't eagerly instantiate singletons
- **Maintains Semantics**: Preserves existing singleton lifetime and
construction order
- **Works Everywhere**: Detects circular dependencies regardless of
ValidateOnBuild
- **Clear Errors**: Developers get actionable error messages with
service type names
- **Thread-Safe**: Thread-local tracking ensures no interference
- **Type Safety**: Uses `HashSet<ServiceCallSite>` with contravariant
comparer

commit.message:
Chapter 19: Learning from Examples — complete interactive visualizations

Implements all algorithms, tests (100% coverage), and 5 interactive
visualization components for Chapter 19.

## Algorithms (src/algorithms/index.ts)
- entropy(p, n) — binary entropy H(q)
- informationGain(attr, examples) — ID3 information gain
- learnDecisionTree(examples, attrs, parent) — recursive ID3
- learnDecisionTreeSteps(examples, attrs) — step-by-step ID3 trace
- linearRegressionGD(data, lr, epochs) — batch gradient descent
- euclideanDistance(a, b) — L2 distance
- knnClassify(training, query, k) — k-NN plurality classifier
- perceptronLearn(data, lr, maxEpochs) — online perceptron
- adaBoost(data, rounds) — AdaBoost with decision stumps

## Tests (66 tests, 100% branch + line coverage)
- Restaurant dataset (12 examples, 10 attributes) for ID3
- Edge cases: empty data, missing attributes, convergence, rounds=0

## Components
- DecisionTreeVisualizer: SVG step-by-step ID3 + what-if example toggles
- BiasVarianceDemo: polynomial fitting (degrees 1–12) + noise slider
- LinearRegressionVisualizer: clickable scatter + gradient descent animation
- KNNVisualizer: 2D scatter + decision boundary grid + neighbor lines
- EnsembleVisualizer: AdaBoost rounds + weighted circles + stump lines

## App.tsx
9-section multi-page app with sticky tab navigation covering all of
Chapter 19 (19.1 Forms of Learning through 19.9 Developing ML Systems).

commit.message:
Fix invisible cursor: remove non-parity WM_SETCURSOR handler

The decomp does NOT handle WM_SETCURSOR (0x20) - it falls through to
DefWindowProcA which uses the class cursor set by SetClassLongA(GCL_HCURSOR).

Our non-parity handler checked (is_mouse_on && windows_mouse==0) and called
SetCursor(NULL)+return 1, which hid the cursor. In windowed mode:
- TRIBE_Screen_Game::set_focus() sets windows_mouse=0 when game has focus
- set_mouse_cursor() still set class cursor to IDC_ARROW (custom_mouse==0)
- But our handler intercepted WM_SETCURSOR and nulled it before DefWindowProc

Root cause chain:
1. set_focus() -> windows_mouse=0 (correct parity)
2. WM_SETCURSOR -> our handler -> SetCursor(NULL) (non-parity, blocking DefWindowProc)
3. DefWindowProc never gets to use class cursor -> invisible

Fix: Remove non-parity handler. WM_SETCURSOR now falls to DefWindowProcA which
uses the class cursor (IDC_ARROW) set by set_mouse_cursor/mouse_on.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chapter-04: add 6 new search algorithms (§4.1–§4.5)

Add Local Beam Search, Gradient Descent, AND-OR Search,
Sensorless Belief-State Search, Online DFS Agent, and LRTA*
to chapter-04-search-complex/src/algorithms/index.ts.

Algorithm details:
- localBeamSearch (§4.1.3): k-beam search with deduplication
- gradientDescent (§4.2): minimises f(x)=(x-3)²+2sin(5x)
- andOrSearch (§4.3): erratic vacuum world with path-based
  cycle detection; returns step trace + solution tree
- sensorlessSearch (§4.4): BFS over belief states in the
  deterministic vacuum world; finds [Left,Suck,Right,Suck]
- onlineDFSAgent (§4.5): ONLINE-DFS-AGENT with untried/
  unbacktracked tables and backtracking support
- lrtaStar (§4.5): Learning Real-Time A* with monotone H
  updates; traces Figure 4.23 example s1→…→s7

coverage on src/algorithms/index.ts. Includes targeted

commit.message:
Add Grind 169 functional programming static site — Phase 1 (#1)

* Initial plan

* Add GitHub Pages static site for Grind 169 functional solutions

- index.html: single-page app with hash-based routing (#/problem/:id,
  #/matrix), hero stats (dynamically computed), app bar with nav and
  dark/light toggle
- css/style.css: Material Design-inspired theme, CSS custom properties
  for light/dark modes, responsive layout, difficulty badges, code block
  and syntax-highlight classes, ripple animation
- js/data.js: all 169 Grind problems with id, LC number, title,
  difficulty, acceptance; LANGUAGES array; SOLVED_IDS set
- js/solutions.js: functional solutions for problems 1-5 in all 8
  languages (TypeScript, Haskell, Elixir, Rust, Scala, OCaml,
  Clojure, Common Lisp)
- js/app.js: router, real-time search/filter, language tabs with
  localStorage preference, dark/light mode (localStorage), copy-to-
  clipboard, ripple effects, 169×8 checklist matrix view, simple
  regex-based syntax highlighter (no external deps)
- _config.yml: disables Jekyll theme for GitHub Pages
- .nojekyll: prevents Jekyll processing
